tasks.title: Create Property Management Module
tasks.description: Develop the foundational Property Management Module by extending the SugarBean class with all required fields for real estate listings.
tasks.details: Use Module Builder to scaffold the new 'Properties' module. Create the database table 'properties' with the following fields: MLS ID (Text), Street Address (Text), City (Text), State (Dropdown), Zip Code (Text), Price (Currency), Status (Dropdown: Active, Pending, Sold), Bedrooms (Int), Bathrooms (Decimal), Square Footage (Int), Description (Text Area), Main Photo (Image). Define relationships with Contacts (sellers/buyers) and Opportunities (renamed to Transactions). Implement the vardefs.php file with all field definitions and relationships. Create the basic views (list, detail, edit) using SuiteCRM's view.list.php, view.detail.php, and view.edit.php patterns. Ensure proper ACL controls are set up for the module. Use SuiteCRM 7.x compatible code patterns and follow the MVC structure.
tasks.testStrategy: Test CRUD operations for property records. Verify all fields save and display correctly. Test relationships with Contacts and Opportunities. Ensure list views filter and sort properly. Validate that image uploads for property photos work correctly. Test ACL permissions for different user roles.

subtasks.title: Scaffold Properties Module Using Module Builder
subtasks.description: Use SuiteCRM's Module Builder to create the foundational 'Properties' module, ensuring it extends the SugarBean class.
subtasks.details: Initiate a new module named 'Properties' via Module Builder. Configure it to extend SugarBean, setting up the basic module structure and files required for further customization.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the module appears in the admin panel and can be deployed without errors. Confirm that the generated files follow SuiteCRM 7.x conventions.

subtasks.title: Define Database Table and Fields
subtasks.description: Create the 'properties' database table with all specified fields for real estate listings.
subtasks.details: Add fields: MLS ID, Street Address, City, State (Dropdown), Zip Code, Price (Currency), Status (Dropdown: Active, Pending, Sold), Bedrooms (Int), Bathrooms (Decimal), Square Footage (Int), Description (Text Area), Main Photo (Image) in the module's vardefs.php and ensure the table is created accordingly.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run Quick Repair and Rebuild. Check that all fields exist in the database and can be saved and retrieved via the module.

subtasks.title: Implement Relationships with Contacts and Transactions
subtasks.description: Define and configure relationships between Properties, Contacts (sellers/buyers), and Opportunities (renamed to Transactions).
subtasks.details: Use vardefs.php and relationship metadata to establish many-to-many or one-to-many relationships as appropriate. Ensure Opportunities are relabeled as Transactions throughout the module.
subtasks.testStrategy: Create and link records between Properties, Contacts, and Transactions. Verify relationships display correctly in subpanels and can be navigated bidirectionally.

subtasks.title: Develop Basic Module Views (List, Detail, Edit)
subtasks.description: Create the list, detail, and edit views for the Properties module using SuiteCRM's MVC patterns.
subtasks.details: Implement view.list.php, view.detail.php, and view.edit.php for the module. Ensure all fields are displayed and editable as required, and that image upload for Main Photo is supported.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test each view for correct field rendering, data entry, and image upload. Confirm navigation between views and proper display of related records.

subtasks.title: Configure Access Control and Permissions
subtasks.description: Set up ACL controls for the Properties module to manage user permissions according to SuiteCRM standards.
subtasks.details: Define ACL roles and permissions for the module, ensuring only authorized users can view, edit, or delete property records. Integrate with SuiteCRM's ACL framework.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test CRUD operations with users of different roles. Verify that permissions are enforced for all module actions and views.

tasks.title: Rename Opportunities Module to Transactions
tasks.description: Relabel the existing Opportunities module to 'Transactions' to better reflect real estate terminology.
tasks.details: Modify the language files (en_us.lang.php) in the Opportunities module to change all instances of 'Opportunity' to 'Transaction'. Update the module's display name in modules/Opportunities/language/en_us.lang.php. Modify the module's metadata in modules/Opportunities/metadata/subpanels/ to reflect the new terminology. Add custom fields needed for real estate transactions if not already present. Ensure the sales_stage dropdown options are updated to match the Transaction Pipeline Stages: Inquiry, Showing, Offer Made, Under Contract, Inspection/Appraisal, Clear to Close, Closed. Add a custom field 'commission_c' (Currency) to store calculated commission values. Use Studio to make these changes where possible to ensure proper metadata updates.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify all UI elements show 'Transaction' instead of 'Opportunity'. Test that existing opportunity records are accessible and functional under the new label. Confirm that reports and dashlets referencing opportunities still work correctly. Test that the new sales stages appear correctly in dropdowns and filters.

subtasks.title: Update Module Display Name and Labels
subtasks.description: Change all visible references of 'Opportunities' to 'Transactions' throughout the CRM interface.
subtasks.details:
Use the Admin panel's 'Rename Modules' or 'Module Names and Icons' feature to update both singular and plural labels for the Opportunities module. Ensure the new name appears in navigation, list views, and record views. Edit the en_us.lang.php language file in modules/Opportunities/language/ to replace all instances of 'Opportunity' with 'Transaction'.

tasks.title: Create Mock MLS Database
tasks.description: Create and populate a mock MLS database table to support the CMA Generator feature.
tasks.details: Create a new database table 'mock_mls_data' with fields matching the Properties module plus additional fields for comparable analysis (days on market, price per square foot, etc.). Create an installation script that populates this table with at least 100 sample properties across various neighborhoods, price points, and property types. Ensure data is realistic and varied enough to generate meaningful CMAs. Include fields for: property_id, address, city, state, zip, bedrooms, bathrooms, square_footage, lot_size, year_built, list_price, sold_price, days_on_market, listing_date, sold_date, property_type. Create a Bean class for this table to allow easy querying through SuiteCRM's ORM. Add indexes on commonly queried fields (zip, price range, bedrooms, bathrooms) for performance.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify the table is created with all required fields. Confirm sample data is properly inserted. Test queries to retrieve comparable properties based on location, size, and price criteria. Validate that the data can be effectively used by the CMA Generator. Test performance of queries with different filter combinations.

subtasks.title: Design Mock MLS Database Schema
subtasks.description: Define the structure of the 'mock_mls_data' table, ensuring all required fields for property and comparable analysis are included.
subtasks.details: List all necessary fields: property_id, address, city, state, zip, bedrooms, bathrooms, square_footage, lot_size, year_built, list_price, sold_price, days_on_market, listing_date, sold_date, property_type. Specify data types and constraints. Identify which fields require indexing for performance.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review schema for completeness and correctness. Validate field types and constraints. Confirm that all fields required for CMA generation are present.

subtasks.title: Implement Database Table and Indexes
subtasks.description: Create the 'mock_mls_data' table in the database according to the defined schema and add indexes on commonly queried fields.
subtasks.details: Write SQL or use SuiteCRM's vardefs to create the table. Add indexes on zip, price range, bedrooms, and bathrooms fields to optimize query performance.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify table creation in the database. Check that all indexes are present and correctly configured. Test basic insert and select operations.

subtasks.title: Develop Data Population Script
subtasks.description: Create an installation script to populate the 'mock_mls_data' table with at least 100 realistic and varied sample property records.
subtasks.details: Generate sample data covering diverse neighborhoods, price points, and property types. Ensure data variation in days on market, price per square foot, and other key fields. Script should be idempotent and rerunnable.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run the script and confirm that at least 100 records are inserted. Review data for realism and diversity. Ensure rerunning the script does not create duplicates.

subtasks.title: Create SuiteCRM Bean Class for Mock MLS Table
subtasks.description: Develop a Bean class for the 'mock_mls_data' table to enable ORM-based querying within SuiteCRM.
subtasks.details: Define the Bean class with all fields mapped. Register the Bean in SuiteCRM so it can be used for data access and manipulation via SuiteCRM's ORM.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test CRUD operations using the Bean. Validate that all fields are accessible and queries return expected results.

subtasks.title: Validate Data Usability and Performance
subtasks.description: Test the mock MLS data for compatibility with the CMA Generator and assess query performance on key fields.
subtasks.details: Execute sample CMA queries filtering by location, size, and price. Measure query response times and confirm that indexes improve performance. Ensure data supports meaningful CMA generation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run CMA Generator with the mock data. Validate accuracy and relevance of results. Benchmark query performance and adjust indexes if needed.

tasks.title: Implement Commission Calculator Logic Hook
tasks.description: Create a before_save Logic Hook on the Opportunities module to automatically calculate agent commissions when a deal is marked as closed.
tasks.details: Create a custom Logic Hook in custom/modules/Opportunities/logic_hooks.php that triggers on before_save. Implement the hook function to check if sales_stage is changing to 'Closed Won' (or the renamed equivalent). When triggered, calculate the commission based on the amount field and a configurable commission rate (stored in config or as a user preference). Store the result in the custom commission_c field. Add configuration options in the admin panel to set default commission rates (e.g., 2.5% or 3%). Include logic to handle split commissions if needed. The hook should follow this pseudocode: if (bean.sales_stage == 'Closed Won' && bean.sales_stage_changed) { bean.commission_c = bean.amount * (bean.commission_rate / 100); }. Use SuiteCRM's BeanFactory to ensure proper saving.
tasks.testStrategy: Test that commission is correctly calculated when a transaction is moved to Closed Won. Verify that changing other fields doesn't trigger recalculation. Test with different commission rates and transaction amounts. Verify the commission appears correctly on the detail view. Test edge cases like zero amount or null commission rate.

subtasks.title: Register before_save Logic Hook for Opportunities
subtasks.description: Set up the logic_hooks.php file in custom/modules/Opportunities to register a before_save logic hook that will trigger custom commission calculation logic.
subtasks.details: Create or update custom/modules/Opportunities/logic_hooks.php to include an entry for the before_save event, specifying the order, description, file path, class, and method for the commission calculation logic.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that the logic hook is triggered when an Opportunity record is saved by adding temporary logging or debugging output.

subtasks.title: Implement Commission Calculation Logic
subtasks.description: Develop the PHP class and method that performs commission calculation when the sales_stage changes to 'Closed Won'.
subtasks.details: Create the referenced PHP file and class. In the hook method, check if sales_stage is changing to 'Closed Won' (or equivalent). If so, calculate the commission using the amount and commission rate, and set the commission_c field.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that commission_c is only set when sales_stage transitions to 'Closed Won' and remains unchanged for other updates.

subtasks.title: Add Configurable Commission Rate Management
subtasks.description: Implement logic to retrieve the commission rate from configuration or user preferences, and provide admin UI for setting default rates.
subtasks.details: Store the commission rate in a config file or user preference. Add admin panel options to set and update the default commission rate (e.g., 2.5% or 3%). Ensure the logic hook retrieves the correct rate for each calculation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test commission calculation with different configured rates and verify the admin UI updates and persists the rate correctly.

subtasks.title: Implement Split Commission Handling
subtasks.description: Extend the commission calculation logic to support split commissions among multiple agents if applicable.
subtasks.details: Modify the logic hook to detect and handle scenarios where commissions are split, distributing the calculated amount according to defined rules or fields.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with Opportunities involving multiple agents and verify that commission_c is correctly split and recorded for each agent.

subtasks.title: Validate and Test Commission Logic Hook Functionality
subtasks.description: Perform comprehensive testing to ensure the logic hook works as intended, including edge cases and UI integration.
subtasks.details: Test commission calculation for various Opportunity scenarios, including different sales stages, amounts, commission rates, and split commissions. Verify that the commission_c field displays correctly in the detail view and that no unintended recalculations occur.
subtasks.testStrategy: Follow the provided test strategy: test transitions to 'Closed Won', changes to other fields, different rates and amounts, UI display, and edge cases such as zero or missing values.

tasks.title: Develop Transaction Pipeline Kanban View
tasks.description: Create a custom Kanban-style board view for the Transactions module to visualize the deal pipeline.
tasks.details: Create a new view file custom/modules/Opportunities/views/view.kanban.php that extends SugarView. Implement a custom controller method to fetch transactions grouped by sales_stage. Create a Smarty template for the Kanban layout using Bootstrap 3 (compatible with SuiteCRM 7.x) for the column structure. Integrate jKanban (v1.0.0 or compatible version) for the interactive board functionality. Create a custom API endpoint (custom/modules/Opportunities/api/KanbanApi.php) to handle AJAX requests for updating transaction stages. Implement drag-and-drop functionality to move transactions between pipeline stages. Add a button in the Transactions module to switch between list view and Kanban view. Include summary statistics at the top of each column (count and total value). Style the cards to show key transaction information (property address, client name, amount, days in stage).
tasks.testStrategy: Test loading the Kanban view with various numbers of transactions. Verify transactions appear in the correct pipeline stages. Test dragging transactions between stages and confirm the database is updated correctly. Test filtering and searching within the Kanban view. Verify performance with a large number of transactions. Test on different browsers and screen sizes.

subtasks.title: Create Kanban View Controller and Data Fetching Logic
subtasks.description: Develop a new view file (custom/modules/Opportunities/views/view.kanban.php) extending SugarView, and implement a controller method to fetch transactions grouped by sales_stage.
subtasks.details:
Set up the PHP controller to retrieve Opportunities records, grouping them by the sales_stage dropdown. Ensure the data structure supports Kanban columns and cards, and is optimized for AJAX consumption.

subtasks.title: Integrate jKanban for Interactive Drag-and-Drop Functionality
subtasks.description: Integrate jKanban (v1.0.0 or compatible) into the Kanban view to enable drag-and-drop movement of transaction cards between pipeline stages.
subtasks.details:
Configure jKanban to recognize columns as sales stages and cards as transactions. Implement event handlers to trigger AJAX updates when cards are moved between columns.
<info added on 2025-07-25T19:16:49.862Z>
Implemented native drag-and-drop functionality without requiring jKanban library:

subtasks.testStrategy: Test dragging cards between columns and ensure UI updates instantly. Confirm that drag-and-drop works on all supported browsers.

subtasks.title: Develop Custom API Endpoint for Stage Updates
subtasks.description: Create a custom API endpoint (custom/modules/Opportunities/api/KanbanApi.php) to handle AJAX requests for updating transaction sales_stage when cards are moved.
subtasks.details:
Implement secure server-side logic to update the sales_stage field for a transaction when a card is moved. Return updated data for UI refresh and handle error cases gracefully.
<info added on 2025-07-25T19:17:28.995Z>
The custom API endpoint implementation has been integrated directly into the view controller instead of creating a separate API file. The action_updateStage() method in view.kanban.php handles all stage update functionality following SuiteCRM's MVC pattern. This method processes AJAX requests when cards are moved on the Kanban board, performs ACL security checks, validates required parameters (opportunity_id and new_stage), updates the sales_stage field with appropriate probability adjustments, and returns JSON responses with success/failure status. Error handling is implemented for missing records and access denied scenarios. The JavaScript in kanban.js interacts with this endpoint through AJAX calls when users drag and drop cards between columns.
</info added on 2025-07-25T19:17:28.995Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that moving a card triggers an API call and updates the database correctly. Verify error handling for invalid requests and permissions.

tasks.title: Create Open House Management EntryPoint
tasks.description: Develop a custom EntryPoint to provide a public-facing digital sign-in form for open houses that creates leads in the CRM.
tasks.details: Create a new EntryPoint in custom/EntryPoint/OpenHouseSignIn.php registered in custom/Extension/application/Ext/EntryPointRegistry/OpenHouse.php. Design a mobile-friendly HTML form using Bootstrap that collects visitor information (name, email, phone, notes). Create a secure token system to associate the form with a specific property listing. Implement server-side validation for all form inputs. When submitted, create a new Lead record with a relationship to the property. Set the lead_source to 'Open House'. Send an email notification to the agent when a new lead signs in. Create a QR code generator that agents can use to create a property-specific sign-in link. Implement rate limiting to prevent form spam. The URL format should be: index.php?entryPoint=OpenHouseSignIn&property_id=XXX&token=YYY.
tasks.testStrategy: Test form submission with valid and invalid data. Verify leads are created correctly with all submitted information. Test the relationship between leads and properties. Verify email notifications are sent to agents. Test the form on various mobile devices and browsers. Test security measures against common attacks (CSRF, XSS, SQL injection).

subtasks.title: Develop and Register Custom EntryPoint
subtasks.description: Create a new EntryPoint file for the open house sign-in form and register it within the SuiteCRM EntryPoint registry.
subtasks.details: Implement custom/EntryPoint/OpenHouseSignIn.php and register it in custom/Extension/application/Ext/EntryPointRegistry/OpenHouse.php to enable public access to the sign-in form.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the EntryPoint is accessible via the specified URL format and is properly registered in the system.

subtasks.title: Design Mobile-Friendly Sign-In Form
subtasks.description: Build a responsive HTML form using Bootstrap to collect visitor information including name, email, phone, and notes.
subtasks.details: Ensure the form is optimized for mobile devices and provides a user-friendly interface for open house attendees.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test the form layout and usability across multiple mobile devices and browsers.

subtasks.title: Implement Secure Token and Property Association
subtasks.description: Create a secure token system to associate each sign-in form instance with a specific property listing.
subtasks.details: Generate and validate tokens to ensure each form submission is linked to the correct property and prevent unauthorized access.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test token generation, validation, and ensure submissions are only accepted with valid tokens and property IDs.

subtasks.title: Handle Form Submission and Lead Creation
subtasks.description: Process form submissions with server-side validation, create new Lead records in the CRM, relate them to the property, set lead_source to 'Open House', and send agent notifications.
subtasks.details: Validate all input fields, create the Lead record with the appropriate relationship, and trigger an email notification to the assigned agent.
subtasks.testStrategy: Submit valid and invalid data, verify lead creation, property relationship, lead_source value, and email delivery.

subtasks.title: Integrate QR Code Generation and Rate Limiting
subtasks.description: Develop a QR code generator for property-specific sign-in links and implement rate limiting to prevent spam submissions.
subtasks.details: Allow agents to generate QR codes for each property and enforce submission limits to protect against abuse.
subtasks.testStrategy: Generate QR codes, scan and test sign-in links, and verify rate limiting by simulating rapid or repeated submissions.

tasks.title: Implement CMA Generator
tasks.description: Create a Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) generator that produces a client-ready PDF showing the subject property and comparable properties.
tasks.details: Create a custom action in the Properties module (custom/modules/Properties/actions/GenerateCMA.php). Implement logic to query the mock_mls_data table for 3-5 comparable properties based on location, size, and features. Use TCPDF (already included in SuiteCRM) to generate a professional PDF report. Design a template for the PDF that includes: agent branding, subject property details, comparable property details with photos, price comparison chart, and market analysis summary. Add a custom button to the Property detail view to generate the CMA. Include configuration options for CMA parameters (e.g., search radius, price range percentage). Store generated CMAs as Documents linked to both the Property and related Contacts. Implement this pseudocode: function findComparables($property) { return $db->query("SELECT * FROM mock_mls_data WHERE zip = '{$property->zip}' AND bedrooms BETWEEN {$property->bedrooms-1} AND {$property->bedrooms+1} AND bathrooms BETWEEN {$property->bathrooms-1} AND {$property->bathrooms+1} AND square_footage BETWEEN {$property->square_footage*0.8} AND {$property->square_footage*1.2} LIMIT 5"); }
tasks.testStrategy: Test CMA generation for various property types and locations. Verify the PDF contains all required sections and formatting is correct. Test with edge cases (no comparables found, missing data). Verify the PDF is correctly stored as a Document. Test performance with large property datasets. Verify the PDF renders correctly when viewed or printed.

subtasks.title: Develop Custom CMA Generation Action
subtasks.description: Create a custom action (GenerateCMA.php) in the Properties module to initiate CMA generation from the property detail view.
subtasks.details: Implement the backend entry point for CMA generation, ensuring it can be triggered via a custom button in the Property detail view. Register the action in the module menu and controller as needed.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the custom action appears and is accessible from the Property detail view. Confirm the action triggers the CMA generation workflow.

subtasks.title: Implement Comparable Property Query Logic
subtasks.description: Develop logic to query the mock_mls_data table for 3-5 comparable properties based on configurable parameters (location, size, features).
subtasks.details: Use the provided pseudocode as a basis for querying comparables. Add support for configurable search radius and price range percentage. Handle edge cases such as no comparables found.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with various property types and parameter configurations. Ensure the correct number and type of comparables are returned, and handle cases with insufficient data.

subtasks.title: Design and Generate CMA PDF Report
subtasks.description: Use TCPDF to generate a professional CMA PDF including agent branding, subject property details, comparable property details with photos, price comparison chart, and market analysis summary.
subtasks.details: Create a reusable PDF template. Integrate property and comparables data, including images and charts. Ensure the report is client-ready and visually consistent with branding guidelines.
subtasks.testStrategy: Generate sample PDFs for different scenarios. Verify all required sections are present, data is accurate, images and charts render correctly, and formatting meets requirements.

subtasks.title: Integrate CMA Generation Button and Configuration Options
subtasks.description: Add a custom button to the Property detail view to trigger CMA generation and provide UI for configuring CMA parameters.
subtasks.details: Modify the Property detail view layout to include the button. Implement a modal or panel for users to set search radius, price range, and other parameters before generating the CMA.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the button appears and functions correctly. Test parameter configuration UI for usability and correct data passing to the backend.

subtasks.title: Store Generated CMA as Document and Link to Records
subtasks.description: Automatically save the generated CMA PDF as a Document linked to both the Property and related Contacts.
subtasks.details: Implement logic to create a Document record for each generated CMA, associate it with the relevant Property and Contacts, and ensure proper access permissions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that each generated CMA is stored as a Document, linked correctly, and accessible from both Property and Contact records. Verify document metadata and permissions.

tasks.title: Design Real Estate Hub Dashboard Framework
tasks.description: Create the framework for the custom Real Estate Hub dashboard that will replace the default SuiteCRM homepage.
tasks.details: Create a custom controller (custom/modules/Home/controller.php) that overrides the default Home module controller. Create a custom view (custom/modules/Home/views/view.real-estate-hub.php) that extends SugarView. Design a responsive layout using Bootstrap 3 grid system with placeholder widgets for 'My Active Listings,' 'Transaction Pipeline,' and 'Upcoming Showings/Tasks.' Modify the main menu to prioritize Properties, Contacts, and Transactions modules. Create custom Smarty templates for the dashboard layout (custom/modules/Home/tpl/real-estate-hub.tpl). Set up the dashboard as the default landing page after login by modifying config_override.php. Include areas for quick-action buttons (add property, schedule showing, etc.). Create a toggle to switch between the custom dashboard and standard SuiteCRM home view.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify the custom dashboard loads correctly after login. Test responsive behavior on different screen sizes. Confirm navigation menu changes are applied correctly. Test the toggle between custom and standard dashboards. Verify placeholder widgets display correctly before being populated with real data.

subtasks.title: Develop Custom Controller and View for Real Estate Hub
subtasks.description: Create a custom controller (custom/modules/Home/controller.php) to override the default Home module controller and a custom view (custom/modules/Home/views/view.real-estate-hub.php) that extends SugarView for the Real Estate Hub dashboard.
subtasks.details: Implement the controller logic to route dashboard requests to the custom view. Ensure the view is structured to support dynamic widget loading and future extensibility.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that accessing the Home module loads the custom controller and view. Confirm that the default SuiteCRM homepage is replaced by the new dashboard framework.

subtasks.title: Design Responsive Dashboard Layout with Bootstrap 3
subtasks.description: Design a responsive dashboard layout using the Bootstrap 3 grid system, including placeholder widgets for 'My Active Listings,' 'Transaction Pipeline,' and 'Upcoming Showings/Tasks.'
subtasks.details: Structure the dashboard with rows and columns to accommodate widgets. Use Bootstrap classes to ensure responsiveness across devices. Insert placeholder content for each widget area.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test the dashboard layout on multiple screen sizes and devices. Confirm that widgets resize and reposition correctly according to Bootstrap grid rules.

subtasks.title: Create Custom Smarty Templates for Dashboard
subtasks.description: Develop custom Smarty templates (custom/modules/Home/tpl/real-estate-hub.tpl) to define the dashboard's visual structure and integrate with the custom view.
subtasks.details: Implement Smarty template logic to render the Bootstrap-based layout and placeholder widgets. Ensure templates are modular for future widget integration.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that the dashboard renders correctly using the custom template. Check for proper display of all placeholder widgets and layout elements.

subtasks.title: Modify Main Menu and Implement Dashboard Toggle
subtasks.description: Update the main menu to prioritize Properties, Contacts, and Transactions modules, and implement a toggle to switch between the custom dashboard and the standard SuiteCRM home view.
subtasks.details: Edit menu metadata to reorder modules. Add a toggle button or menu item that allows users to switch between the Real Estate Hub and the default dashboard.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm that the main menu displays the prioritized modules. Test the toggle functionality to ensure seamless switching between dashboard views.

subtasks.title: Set Dashboard as Default Landing Page and Add Quick-Action Areas
subtasks.description: Configure the dashboard as the default landing page after login by modifying config_override.php, and add areas for quick-action buttons (e.g., add property, schedule showing).
subtasks.details: Update configuration to redirect users to the custom dashboard upon login. Design and place quick-action button areas within the dashboard layout for common real estate tasks.
subtasks.testStrategy: Log in as a user and verify redirection to the custom dashboard. Test the visibility and accessibility of quick-action buttons. Ensure all navigation and actions function as intended.

tasks.title: Implement Active Listings Widget
tasks.description: Create a widget for the Real Estate Hub that displays the agent's active property listings.
tasks.details: Create a custom dashlet class (custom/modules/Home/Dashlets/ActiveListingsDashlet/ActiveListingsDashlet.php) that extends DashletGeneric. Implement a query to fetch properties with status='Active' related to the current user. Create a Smarty template for the dashlet that displays properties in a card format with key details (address, price, days on market, thumbnail image). Add sorting options (newest, price high/low). Include quick-action buttons for each listing (edit, view, schedule showing). Implement pagination if there are many listings. Add a 'View All' link to the full Properties list view with an active filter applied. Use this query pattern: SELECT p.id, p.name, p.price_c, p.address_c, p.bedrooms_c, p.bathrooms_c, p.square_footage_c, p.days_on_market_c FROM properties p JOIN properties_cstm pc ON p.id = pc.id_c WHERE p.assigned_user_id = '{$current_user->id}' AND pc.status_c = 'Active' ORDER BY p.date_entered DESC LIMIT 5
tasks.testStrategy: Test the widget with various numbers of active listings. Verify all property information displays correctly. Test sorting functionality. Verify quick-action buttons work as expected. Test pagination if implemented. Verify the 'View All' link applies the correct filter to the list view.

subtasks.title: Develop Custom Dashlet Class for Active Listings
subtasks.description: Create a custom dashlet class (ActiveListingsDashlet.php) that extends DashletGeneric to serve as the backend for the Active Listings widget.
subtasks.details: Set up the PHP class in custom/modules/Home/Dashlets/ActiveListingsDashlet/ActiveListingsDashlet.php, ensuring it registers correctly and can be added to dashboards.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the dashlet appears in the dashboard add menu and loads without errors.

subtasks.title: Implement Query Logic for Fetching Active Listings
subtasks.description: Write and integrate the SQL query to fetch properties with status='Active' assigned to the current user, limiting results and supporting sorting.
subtasks.details: Use the provided query pattern to retrieve property details, and implement sorting options (newest, price high/low) and pagination logic as needed.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with different users and listing counts; verify correct records, sorting, and pagination.

subtasks.title: Design Smarty Template for Card-Based Listing Display
subtasks.description: Create a Smarty template to display each property as a card with key details (address, price, days on market, thumbnail image) and quick-action buttons.
subtasks.details: Develop the template to show all required fields, ensure responsive layout, and include buttons for edit, view, and schedule showing.
subtasks.testStrategy: Check visual layout with various data; verify all fields and buttons render and function as intended.

subtasks.title: Integrate 'View All' Link and Active Filter
subtasks.description: Add a 'View All' link to the dashlet that navigates to the Properties list view with an active status filter applied.
subtasks.details: Ensure the link passes the correct filter parameters so the Properties module displays only active listings for the current user.
subtasks.testStrategy: Click the link and confirm the Properties list view loads with the correct filter and results.

subtasks.title: Test and Validate Widget Functionality
subtasks.description: Perform comprehensive testing of the widget, including edge cases, to ensure all features work as specified.
subtasks.details: Test with varying numbers of listings, sorting, pagination, quick-action buttons, and the 'View All' link. Validate display and interaction across supported browsers.
subtasks.testStrategy: Follow the provided test strategy: verify display, sorting, actions, pagination, and filter application. Document and resolve any issues found.

tasks.title: Implement Transaction Pipeline Widget
tasks.description: Create a widget for the Real Estate Hub that provides a summary view of the agent's transaction pipeline.
tasks.details: Create a custom dashlet class (custom/modules/Home/Dashlets/PipelineDashlet/PipelineDashlet.php) that extends DashletGeneric. Implement a query to count transactions by stage and calculate total value for each stage. Create a Smarty template that displays a horizontal pipeline visualization with counts and values for each stage. Use Bootstrap progress bars or a simple chart library (Chart.js 2.9.x, compatible with SuiteCRM 7.x) to visualize the pipeline distribution. Include a summary of total pipeline value and count. Add a link to the full Kanban view of the pipeline. Implement a refresh button to update the data without reloading the page. Use color coding to highlight stages with high value or that require attention. The widget should be compact but informative, focusing on key metrics rather than individual transaction details.
tasks.testStrategy: Test the widget with transactions in various stages. Verify counts and calculations are correct. Test the visualization with different distribution patterns. Verify the link to the Kanban view works correctly. Test the refresh functionality. Verify the widget updates when transactions change stages.

subtasks.title: Develop Custom Dashlet Class for Pipeline Widget
subtasks.description: Create the PipelineDashlet class in custom/modules/Home/Dashlets/PipelineDashlet/PipelineDashlet.php, extending DashletGeneric, to serve as the backend logic for the transaction pipeline widget.
subtasks.details: Set up the PHP class structure, ensure it integrates with SuiteCRM's dashlet framework, and prepare for data querying and widget configuration.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the dashlet appears in the dashboard add-widget menu and loads without errors.

subtasks.title: Implement Transaction Stage Query and Aggregation Logic
subtasks.description: Develop logic within the dashlet class to query transactions, count them by stage, and calculate the total value for each stage.
subtasks.details: Write efficient SQL or ORM queries to fetch transaction data grouped by stage, and aggregate both count and value per stage for the current agent.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with sample data to ensure counts and values are accurate for each stage, including edge cases with no transactions or all in one stage.

subtasks.title: Design and Implement Smarty Template for Visualization
subtasks.description: Create a Smarty template to display a horizontal pipeline visualization, showing counts and values per stage, using Bootstrap progress bars or Chart.js 2.9.x.
subtasks.details: Develop a compact, visually clear template that highlights key metrics, uses color coding for high-value or attention-required stages, and includes a summary of total pipeline value and count.
subtasks.testStrategy: Render the widget with various data distributions and verify correct visualization, color coding, and summary display.

subtasks.title: Add Interactive Features: Kanban Link and Refresh Button
subtasks.description: Integrate a link to the full Kanban pipeline view and implement a refresh button that updates the widget data without reloading the page.
subtasks.details: Ensure the Kanban link navigates correctly and the refresh button triggers an AJAX call to reload data, maintaining the widget's compact layout.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test navigation to the Kanban view and verify the refresh button updates data in real time without a full page reload.

subtasks.title: Finalize, Test, and Optimize Widget for Key Metrics
subtasks.description: Conduct comprehensive testing, optimize performance, and ensure the widget focuses on summary metrics rather than individual transaction details.
subtasks.details: Test with transactions in various stages, validate calculations, check responsiveness, and confirm that the widget remains informative and compact.
subtasks.testStrategy: Perform end-to-end testing with different data scenarios, verify all features, and ensure the widget meets user requirements for clarity and utility.

tasks.title: Implement Upcoming Showings/Tasks Widget
tasks.description: Create a widget for the Real Estate Hub that displays upcoming showings and important tasks.
tasks.details: Create a custom dashlet class (custom/modules/Home/Dashlets/UpcomingDashlet/UpcomingDashlet.php) that extends DashletGeneric. Implement a query to fetch upcoming meetings (showings) and tasks due in the next 7 days. Create a Smarty template that displays these items in a timeline or list format. Group items by day (Today, Tomorrow, This Week). Include key details for each item (time, property/client, type). Add quick-action buttons to complete tasks or reschedule meetings. Implement color coding based on priority or proximity to due date. Add a 'View Calendar' link to the full calendar view. Include a count of overdue items with a warning indicator. Filter to focus on property showings and transaction-related tasks by default. Use this query pattern for meetings: SELECT m.id, m.name, m.date_start, m.status, p.name as property_name FROM meetings m LEFT JOIN meetings_properties_c mp ON m.id = mp.meeting_id LEFT JOIN properties p ON p.id = mp.property_id WHERE m.assigned_user_id = '{$current_user->id}' AND m.date_start > NOW() AND m.date_start < DATE_ADD(NOW(), INTERVAL 7 DAY) ORDER BY m.date_start ASC
tasks.testStrategy: Test the widget with various combinations of upcoming meetings and tasks. Verify items are grouped correctly by day. Test quick-action buttons functionality. Verify overdue items are highlighted appropriately. Test with items that have and don't have related properties. Verify the 'View Calendar' link works correctly.

subtasks.title: Develop Custom Dashlet Class for Upcoming Showings and Tasks
subtasks.description: Create the UpcomingDashlet class in custom/modules/Home/Dashlets/UpcomingDashlet/UpcomingDashlet.php, extending DashletGeneric to support upcoming showings and tasks.
subtasks.details: Set up the PHP class structure, initialize dashlet configuration, and ensure it can be added to dashboards. Prepare for data querying and template integration.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the dashlet appears in the dashboard add menu and loads without errors.

subtasks.title: Implement Data Queries for Upcoming Meetings and Tasks
subtasks.description: Write queries to fetch upcoming meetings (showings) and tasks due in the next 7 days, filtered for property showings and transaction-related tasks.
subtasks.details: Use the provided SQL pattern for meetings and create a similar query for tasks. Ensure results include required fields (time, property/client, type) and filter by assigned user.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test queries directly and within the dashlet to confirm correct filtering and data retrieval for various user scenarios.

subtasks.title: Design Smarty Template for Timeline/List Display
subtasks.description: Create a Smarty template to display upcoming items in a grouped timeline or list format, showing key details and grouping by day (Today, Tomorrow, This Week).
subtasks.details: Implement grouping logic, display item details, add color coding for priority/proximity, and include a count of overdue items with a warning indicator.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify correct grouping, color coding, and overdue indicators with a variety of test data.

subtasks.title: Add Quick-Action Buttons and Calendar Integration
subtasks.description: Implement quick-action buttons for completing tasks and rescheduling meetings, and add a 'View Calendar' link to the full calendar view.
subtasks.details: Ensure actions update records in real time and the calendar link navigates to the correct view. Handle UI feedback for actions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test all quick-action buttons for correct behavior and confirm the calendar link works as intended.

subtasks.title: Implement Filtering, Default Focus, and Finalize Widget
subtasks.description: Apply default filters to focus on property showings and transaction-related tasks, finalize dashlet configuration, and ensure all requirements are met.
subtasks.details: Review and refine filtering logic, confirm all display and interaction requirements, and prepare for deployment.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test the widget end-to-end with different user roles and data sets to ensure all features and filters work as specified.

tasks.title: Customize Main Navigation Menu
tasks.description: Modify the main SuiteCRM navigation to prioritize real estate-specific modules and simplify the user experience.
tasks.details: Create a custom extension file (custom/Extension/application/Ext/Menu/real_estate_menu.php) to modify the top navigation menu. Reorder modules to prioritize Properties, Contacts, and Transactions. Remove or deprioritize modules not relevant to real estate agents. Add custom menu items for quick access to frequently used features (Add Property, Schedule Showing, Generate CMA). Implement a simplified menu structure with dropdown categories if needed. Use CSS to style the menu with real estate-themed colors and icons. Ensure the menu is responsive and works well on mobile devices. Consider implementing a collapsible sidebar navigation as an alternative to the top menu for better space utilization. Use SuiteCRM's built-in menu extension points to ensure compatibility with future updates.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify the menu displays the correct modules in the specified order. Test navigation to all key modules and features. Test responsive behavior on different screen sizes. Verify custom menu items link to the correct actions. Test with different user roles to ensure appropriate access controls are maintained.

subtasks.title: Analyze and Prioritize Real Estate Modules
subtasks.description: Identify all modules relevant to real estate workflows and determine their priority order for the main navigation menu, focusing on Properties, Contacts, and Transactions.
subtasks.details: Review existing SuiteCRM modules and any custom modules. Create a prioritized list with Properties, Contacts, and Transactions at the top. Document modules to remove or deprioritize based on real estate agent needs.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the prioritized list with stakeholders. Ensure all required modules are included and irrelevant modules are identified for removal or deprioritization.

subtasks.title: Develop Custom Menu Extension File
subtasks.description: Create and configure the custom extension file (custom/Extension/application/Ext/Menu/real_estate_menu.php) to implement the new navigation structure.
subtasks.details: Write PHP code to reorder modules, remove or hide non-relevant modules, and add custom menu items for quick access to Add Property, Schedule Showing, and Generate CMA. Use SuiteCRM's menu extension points for compatibility.
subtasks.testStrategy: Check that the menu displays modules in the specified order and that custom menu items appear and link correctly.

subtasks.title: Implement Dropdown Categories and Collapsible Sidebar
subtasks.description: Design and implement a simplified menu structure with dropdown categories and, if needed, a collapsible sidebar navigation for improved usability and space utilization.
subtasks.details: Group related modules and features under dropdown categories. Develop a collapsible sidebar alternative to the top menu, ensuring both options are available and user-friendly.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test navigation flow for clarity and ease of use. Verify dropdowns and sidebar function correctly on all supported devices.

subtasks.title: Apply Real Estate-Themed Styling and Icons
subtasks.description: Style the navigation menu using CSS to reflect real estate branding, including custom colors and icons for key modules and menu items.
subtasks.details: Create or source real estate-themed icons. Update CSS to apply the new color scheme and icons to the navigation menu. Ensure visual consistency with other real estate modules.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that styling and icons display correctly across browsers and devices. Confirm that the menu aligns with branding guidelines.

subtasks.title: Ensure Responsiveness and Role-Based Access
subtasks.description: Optimize the navigation menu for mobile devices and implement access controls so users see only the modules and menu items appropriate for their roles.
subtasks.details: Test and refine the menu layout for various screen sizes. Configure SuiteCRM role-based menu visibility using built-in settings or custom logic as needed.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test menu responsiveness on multiple devices and browsers. Verify that users with different roles see the correct menu items and have appropriate access.

tasks.title: Implement Property-Contact Relationships
tasks.description: Create and configure relationships between Properties, Contacts, and Transactions to establish a complete real estate data model.
tasks.details: Define relationships in vardefs.php files: Properties to Contacts (many-to-many for buyers, sellers, and other roles), Properties to Transactions (one-to-many), and Contacts to Transactions (many-to-many). Create relationship metadata files in custom/Extension/modules/*/Ext/Vardefs/ directories. Implement subpanels in detail views to show related records. Add custom fields to relationship tables to specify roles (e.g., seller, buyer, agent, inspector). Modify the Properties module to allow quick creation of a Transaction from a Property record. Add filters in subpanels to show contacts by role. Ensure proper cascade deletion behavior to maintain data integrity. Create relationship-specific views that show the most relevant fields for each context. Use SuiteCRM's relationship management APIs to ensure proper handling of relationships.
tasks.testStrategy: Test creating and viewing relationships between all entity types. Verify subpanels display related records correctly. Test role-specific filtering in relationship subpanels. Verify cascade deletion behavior works as expected. Test creating a Transaction from a Property record. Verify all custom fields in relationship tables save and display correctly.

subtasks.title: Define and Configure Relationship Metadata
subtasks.description: Create and configure many-to-many and one-to-many relationships between Properties, Contacts, and Transactions in vardefs.php and relationship metadata files.
subtasks.details: Define Properties-Contacts (many-to-many with roles), Properties-Transactions (one-to-many), and Contacts-Transactions (many-to-many) relationships in vardefs.php and custom/Extension/modules/*/Ext/Vardefs/ directories. Ensure relationship tables support custom fields for roles.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify relationships are correctly defined in metadata. Check that relationship tables are created with required fields and custom role columns.

subtasks.title: Implement and Configure Subpanels in Detail Views
subtasks.description: Add and configure subpanels in Properties, Contacts, and Transactions detail views to display related records and enable navigation.
subtasks.details: Configure subpanels to show related Contacts and Transactions in Properties, related Properties and Transactions in Contacts, and related Properties and Contacts in Transactions. Ensure subpanels display key fields and support navigation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that subpanels appear in the correct modules and display the appropriate related records. Verify navigation between related records works as expected.

subtasks.title: Add Custom Fields and Role Filters to Relationship Tables and Subpanels
subtasks.description: Extend relationship tables with custom fields to specify roles (e.g., buyer, seller, agent) and implement subpanel filters to display contacts by role.
subtasks.details: Modify relationship tables to include custom fields for roles. Update subpanel layouts and logic to allow filtering of contacts by their role in the relationship.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that custom role fields are present and editable. Verify subpanel filters correctly display contacts by selected role.

subtasks.title: Enable Quick Transaction Creation from Property Records
subtasks.description: Modify the Properties module to allow users to quickly create a new Transaction directly from a Property record, pre-filling relevant fields.
subtasks.details: Add a button or action in the Property detail view to create a Transaction. Ensure the new Transaction is linked to the Property and pre-populated with relevant data.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test the quick-create functionality from Property records. Verify new Transactions are correctly linked and fields are pre-filled.

subtasks.title: Implement Cascade Deletion and Relationship-Specific Views
subtasks.description: Configure cascade deletion behavior to maintain data integrity and create relationship-specific views showing the most relevant fields for each context.
subtasks.details: Set up cascade deletion rules so that deleting a Property, Contact, or Transaction updates or removes related records as appropriate. Design and implement custom views for each relationship context, displaying the most relevant fields.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test cascade deletion scenarios to ensure data integrity. Verify that relationship-specific views display the correct fields and contextually relevant information.

tasks.title: Create Custom Module Icons and Styling
tasks.description: Design and implement custom icons and styling for the real estate-specific modules to enhance visual recognition and branding.
tasks.details: Create custom SVG icons for Properties, Transactions, and other real estate-specific modules. Design a consistent color scheme that aligns with real estate industry standards. Implement custom CSS in custom/themes/SuiteP/css/real_estate_style.css. Create custom record view icons for different property types and transaction stages. Modify the SuiteCRM theme to incorporate real estate-specific visual elements. Ensure all custom styling is responsive and works across devices. Use icon font libraries like Font Awesome 4.7 (compatible with SuiteCRM 7.x) for consistent iconography. Create a custom logo for the Real Estate Hub dashboard. Implement subtle animations for state changes in the Kanban board. Ensure all styling follows accessibility guidelines for color contrast and readability.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify custom icons display correctly in all contexts (navigation, record views, list views). Test styling across different browsers and devices. Verify color scheme is consistent throughout the application. Test with screen readers and other accessibility tools. Verify custom animations work smoothly and don't impact performance.

subtasks.title: Design Custom SVG Icons and Logo
subtasks.description: Create unique SVG icons for Properties, Transactions, and other real estate modules, as well as a custom logo for the Real Estate Hub dashboard.
subtasks.details: Design icons that visually represent each module and property type, ensuring they are distinct and align with real estate industry aesthetics. Develop a dashboard logo that reinforces branding.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify all SVG icons and the logo render correctly in design previews and are visually distinct for each module.

subtasks.title: Define and Document Real Estate Color Scheme
subtasks.description: Establish a consistent color palette that aligns with real estate industry standards and supports accessibility.
subtasks.details: Select primary, secondary, and accent colors for use across modules and UI elements. Document color codes and ensure sufficient contrast for readability.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test color combinations for accessibility compliance using contrast checking tools and verify consistency across mockups.

subtasks.title: Implement Custom CSS and Icon Fonts
subtasks.description: Develop and apply custom CSS in custom/themes/SuiteP/css/real_estate_style.css, integrating Font Awesome 4.7 for iconography.
subtasks.details: Write CSS rules to style modules, record views, and navigation with the new color scheme and icons. Ensure Font Awesome 4.7 is loaded and used consistently for icon elements.
subtasks.testStrategy: Check that all custom styles and icons appear as intended in navigation, record views, and list views across browsers.

subtasks.title: Enhance SuiteCRM Theme with Real Estate Visual Elements
subtasks.description: Modify the SuiteCRM SuiteP theme to incorporate real estate-specific visual elements, including custom record view icons and Kanban board animations.
subtasks.details: Update theme templates and layouts to display custom icons for property types and transaction stages. Implement subtle CSS animations for Kanban board state changes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that theme changes are visible throughout the application, animations work smoothly, and icons update dynamically based on record type or stage.

subtasks.title: Test Responsiveness and Accessibility
subtasks.description: Ensure all custom icons, styles, and animations are responsive and meet accessibility guidelines.
subtasks.details: Test the UI on various devices and browsers. Use screen readers and accessibility tools to confirm color contrast, icon legibility, and keyboard navigation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate that all visual elements adapt to different screen sizes, pass accessibility checks, and function correctly for users with assistive technologies.

tasks.title: Implement System Integration Tests
tasks.description: Create comprehensive integration tests to verify all components work together correctly and the user flows function as expected.
tasks.details: Develop test scripts for the six core user flows described in the PRD: logging in and viewing the Real Estate Hub, adding a new property listing, managing an open house with lead capture, moving a lead through the Transaction Pipeline, closing a deal and calculating commission, and generating a CMA for a potential seller. Create a test dataset with sample properties, contacts, and transactions. Implement automated tests using PHPUnit where possible. Create manual test scripts with step-by-step instructions for complex workflows. Test cross-module functionality and data consistency. Verify all calculated fields update correctly when related records change. Test performance with realistic data volumes. Create a test report template to document test results. Include edge cases and error handling scenarios in test coverage. Test user roles and permissions to ensure proper access control.
tasks.testStrategy: Execute all automated tests and verify they pass. Perform manual testing of the six core user flows. Verify data integrity across related modules. Test with different user roles to ensure appropriate access controls. Verify performance meets acceptable standards with realistic data volumes. Document any issues found and track their resolution.

subtasks.title: Develop Test Plan and Define Test Cases for Core User Flows
subtasks.description: Create a comprehensive test plan outlining objectives, scope, environments, and schedules. Define detailed test cases for each of the six core user flows described in the PRD, including normal, edge, and error scenarios.
subtasks.details: Document test objectives, scope, and required environments. For each user flow (login/Real Estate Hub, add property, manage open house, transaction pipeline, deal closing/commission, CMA generation), write test cases covering happy path, edge cases, and error handling. Specify data requirements and expected outcomes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review test plan for completeness. Validate that all user flows and scenarios are covered. Ensure test cases are clear, unambiguous, and traceable to requirements.

subtasks.title: Create and Populate Test Dataset
subtasks.description: Design and generate a test dataset with sample properties, contacts, and transactions to support all integration test scenarios.
subtasks.details: Identify data requirements from test cases. Create representative records for properties, contacts, and transactions, including edge cases (e.g., missing data, unusual values). Ensure data supports all user roles and permission scenarios.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify dataset completeness and relevance by mapping data to test cases. Validate data integrity and relationships. Confirm dataset loads correctly in the test environment.

subtasks.title: Implement Automated Integration Tests Using PHPUnit
subtasks.description: Develop automated integration test scripts for the six core user flows and cross-module interactions using PHPUnit.
subtasks.details: Write PHPUnit test scripts to automate as many user flows as possible, focusing on data consistency, calculated fields, and cross-module functionality. Include assertions for expected outcomes and error handling. Integrate tests into the CI/CD pipeline.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run automated tests in a production-like environment. Ensure all tests pass and failures are reported with actionable details. Monitor test coverage and maintainability.

subtasks.title: Develop Manual Test Scripts for Complex and Edge Case Workflows
subtasks.description: Create detailed manual test scripts with step-by-step instructions for workflows not fully automatable, including complex user interactions and edge cases.
subtasks.details: Identify workflows requiring manual testing (e.g., multi-step user interactions, UI-driven processes). Write clear, reproducible instructions for testers. Include steps for verifying user roles, permissions, and error handling.
subtasks.testStrategy: Execute manual scripts and document results. Validate that instructions are clear and outcomes are consistent across testers. Update scripts based on feedback.

subtasks.title: Execute Integration Tests, Validate Results, and Document Findings
subtasks.description: Run all automated and manual integration tests, verify system behavior, and document results using a standardized test report template.
subtasks.details: Execute the full suite of integration tests, including performance and access control scenarios. Track defects and unexpected behaviors. Complete test reports detailing coverage, results, and issues found.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review test reports for completeness and accuracy. Ensure all critical paths, edge cases, and error scenarios are validated. Confirm that defects are logged and tracked for resolution.
